 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.4","title":"Task: Status bar redesign + toast notifications","description":"Goal
- Make status feedback feel premium and always keep key hints visible.

Background
- Current status bar hides key hints whenever any status message is present, and all messages are styled the same.
- Skill guidance recommends a composited status bar with distinct chips and toast notifications.

Details
- Redesign status bar into segments: left (mode/context such as provider/profile/project), center (status message), right (key hints).
- Use keycap styles from the theme system for all hints.
- Add severity styling: success/warn/error colors and icons; keep hints visible even with messages.
- Add a toast system for transient success/errors (top-right or bottom-right), with timeout.
- Provide a config toggle for toasts and reduced motion.

Tests
- Unit tests for segment composition, severity styling, and hint persistence.
- Golden snapshots for status bar and toasts in light/dark themes.
- E2E TUI test that triggers success/error messages and verifies hints remain visible.
- Emit structured test logs with severity, message length, and toast timing.

Success
- Users always see navigation hints and can immediately distinguish error/success states.
","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-fnkk (existing status color task).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:45.147497750Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T02:17:25.972174613Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.4","depends_on_id":"caam-fnkk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:54.254383192Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.4","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:45.148705905Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.4","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:49.924862011Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.4","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:34:50.781325107Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.5","title":"Task: Search bar + command palette UX","description":"Goal
- Make search/filtering visible and intuitive, and add a command palette for power users.

Background
- Current search mode has no visible input; users type blindly and rely on status text.
- Skill guidance highlights list filtering and command palette patterns.

Details
- Add a visible search bar component (textinput) that appears when search mode is active.
- Show live filter results, match counts, and a clear esc-to-cancel hint in the UI.
- Add a command palette (list component with filtering) presented as a modal overlay for actions like backup/activate/sync/import/export.
- Ensure search state is obvious in both compact and full layouts.
- Provide consistent keybindings for entering/leaving search and palette.

Success
- Users can discover and use search without guesswork; power users can execute actions quickly.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system), caam-bxwa (search indicator task), and caam-l19o.1.3 (modal overlay for command palette).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:02.835728145Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:21:13.067291787Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.5","depends_on_id":"caam-bxwa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:11.330129088Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.5","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:02.836977408Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.5","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:07.979665683Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.5","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:20:57.751497795Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.6","title":"Task: Motion for loading/progress (spinners + progress bars)","description":"Goal
- Add tasteful motion for loading and long-running operations to improve perceived performance.

Background
- Current loading states are static text (Loading…, Syncing…).
- Skill guidance recommends spinners/progress for async operations.

Details
- Introduce bubbles/spinner for sync, usage, import/export preview, and watch events.
- Add progress bars for long operations (bundle export/import, sync steps) when counts are known.
- Ensure motion is disabled when NO_COLOR or reduced-motion config is set.
- Centralize spinner and progress styles in the theme layer for consistency.

Success
- Users always see activity feedback; UI feels alive without being noisy.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-93pq (existing spinner task).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:20.569421371Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:23:20.009911577Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-93pq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:29.594022908Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:20.570525751Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:26.171206211Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:34:53.595060378Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"caam-l19o.1.7","title":"Task: Help/onboarding refresh with Glamour","description":"Goal
- Provide a polished, discoverable help experience with Markdown rendering and contextual shortcuts.

Background
- Current help view is functional but plain; skill guidance recommends Glamour for rich markdown help and structured key hints.

Details
- Use Glamour to render a concise help page (shortcuts + workflows).
- Cache rendered markdown to avoid re-rendering every frame.
- Add contextual help: show relevant hints based on current panel/state.
- Keep short help always visible in the status bar; full help via ? toggles.
- Ensure help is readable in light/dark themes and in NO_COLOR mode.

Success
- New users can learn the TUI in under a minute without leaving the app.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-s5bk (breadcrumbs/context UX).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:37.360331617Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:21:21.542509764Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.7","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:37.361991573Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.7","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:40.878804784Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.7","depends_on_id":"caam-s5bk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:46.754970484Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.8","title":"Task: Focus management + active panel highlighting","description":"Goal
- Make it obvious which panel is active and improve keyboard navigation clarity.

Background
- The UI currently uses selection highlights but lacks a strong focus ring for active panel/region.
- Skill guidance recommends clear focus cues and keyboard hints.

Details
- Add a focus state for provider/profiles/detail panels (border accent, title highlight).
- Show active panel name in the status bar and contextual shortcuts using keycap styles.
- Ensure tab/shift+tab cycles focus in a predictable order.
- Keep focus visibility in both compact and full layouts.

Success
- Users always know where their input is going and how to switch focus.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-l19o.1.2 (layout).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:53.867797053Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:23:54.795553576Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.8","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:53.869090629Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.8","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:12:57.813704114Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.8","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:01.252470419Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.9","title":"Task: Mouse + viewport support for long content","description":"Goal
- Add mouse support and smooth scrolling for long lists and detail content.

Background
- Skill guidance recommends mouse support and viewport for long content to feel premium.

Details
- Enable tea.WithMouseCellMotion in TUI Run and handle mouse wheel for scrolling.
- Use viewport for long detail descriptions and help content.
- Highlight rows on hover where feasible without breaking keyboard navigation.
- Provide config to disable mouse support for minimal terminals.

Success
- Users can scroll and interact with the TUI using mouse in modern terminals.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.2 (layout) and caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:08.455691417Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:22:07.857086136Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.9","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:08.457377292Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.9","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:15.457495418Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.9","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:13:11.997270089Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l3w7","title":"Unit tests for internal/authpool package","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for internal/authpool Package

## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for the AuthPool manager, token monitor loop, and profile state management.

## Test Categories

### 1. Pool Initialization Tests
```go
func TestPoolCreation(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Creating empty pool")
    pool := authpool.NewAuthPool(vault)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Pool created with %d profiles", pool.Count())
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Adding profile")
    err := pool.AddProfile("claude", "alice@gmail.com")
    t.Logf("[TEST] Add result: err=%v", err)
}
```

### 2. Token Expiry Detection Tests
- Token expires in future (should be Ready)
- Token expires soon (should trigger refresh)
- Token already expired (should be Expired)
- Token with no expiry info (should be Unknown)

### 3. Refresh Logic Tests
```go
func TestRefreshTrigger(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Setting up profile with 10min TTL")
    profile := &PooledProfile{
        TokenExpiry: time.Now().Add(10 * time.Minute),
    }
    
    pool := NewAuthPool(WithRefreshThreshold(15 * time.Minute))
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Checking if refresh needed")
    needsRefresh := pool.NeedsRefresh(profile)
    t.Logf("[TEST] TTL=10min, Threshold=15min, NeedsRefresh=%v", needsRefresh)
    
    assert.True(t, needsRefresh) // 10min < 15min threshold
}
```

### 4. Concurrent Access Tests
- Multiple goroutines reading state
- Concurrent refresh triggers
- Race condition detection with -race flag

### 5. Monitor Loop Tests
- Loop starts and stops cleanly
- Refresh triggers at correct intervals
- Context cancellation stops loop
- Error recovery (single profile failure doesnt crash loop)

### 6. Profile State Transitions
```
Unknown -> Checking -> Ready
Unknown -> Checking -> Expired -> Refreshing -> Ready
Unknown -> Checking -> Expired -> Refreshing -> Error
Ready -> Expired (TTL passed)
Refreshing -> Ready (success)
Refreshing -> Error (failure)
Error -> Refreshing (retry)
```

## Logging Requirements
Each test MUST log:
- Initial state
- Action being taken
- State after action
- Time taken for operations
- Any errors with full context

## Mock Components
```go
type MockRefresher struct {
    refreshDelay time.Duration
    shouldFail   bool
    callCount    int
}

func (m *MockRefresher) Refresh(ctx context.Context, profile string) error {
    m.callCount++
    time.Sleep(m.refreshDelay)
    if m.shouldFail {
        return errors.New("mock refresh failure")
    }
    return nil
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All state transitions tested
- [ ] Concurrent access is safe (passes -race)
- [ ] Monitor loop is tested
- [ ] Mock refresher allows failure injection
- [ ] 90%+ code coverage

## Files to Create
- internal/authpool/pool_test.go
- internal/authpool/monitor_test.go
- internal/authpool/mock_test.go
